name: Gipuzkoan Basque (Gipuzkera)

ancestors.notes: Gipuzkoan is the closest living dialect to Euskara Batua and served as the primary model for standardization; grapheme mappings are nearly identical to the standard.

notes: Gipuzkoan (Gipuzkera). Central Basque dialect spoken in Gipuzkoa province, closest to Euskara Batua and the primary reference variety for standardization. Full three-way sibilant contrast maintained (apical /s̺/, laminal /s̻/, postalveolar /ʃ/). Palatal stops [c, ɟ] well preserved in all positions. Aspiration (/h/) is present but variable, often weakening in spontaneous speech. Gipuzkoan prosody exhibits a pitch-accent system distinct from the stress-accent of Biscayan.

sources.author: Hualde, J.I. & Ortiz de Urbina, J. (eds.)
sources.title: A Grammar of Basque

sources.author: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I.
sources.title: The Sounds of the World's Languages
